claim: Plan Mode in Claude Code should be used for 80%+ of sessions; locking in a good plan before execution makes subsequent building nearly automatic
actionable: Start every Claude Code session in plan mode; iterate on the plan until satisfied before triggering implementation
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Plan Mode helps save bad prompts by asking clarifying follow-up questions, improving the likelihood of correct implementation
actionable: Use Plan Mode as a safety net; don't accept vague plans; request clarifications before building
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: AI output quality depends entirely on input quality; vague prompts with missing context yield poor results regardless of model capability
actionable: Invest time in clear, precise prompts with full context; this directly impacts code quality and reduces iteration cycles
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: You should review and potentially edit Plan Mode's proposed plan before accepting; don't blindly accept automated plans
actionable: Always review plans critically; edit and refine before accepting; treat the AI's plan as a draft, not final
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Use custom agents (sub-agents with dedicated context windows) to specialize in specific tasks like documentation exploration, saving tokens in main context
actionable: Build task-specific agents (e.g., Docs Explorer); delegate specialized work to reduce main context load
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Claude Skills document project-specific best practices and patterns; they are lazily loaded only when needed, reducing context bloat
actionable: Create project-specific skills for frameworks, patterns, and conventions; these guide Claude without consuming full context upfront
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Be explicit rather than implicit; explicitly tell Claude to use specific agents/tools rather than hoping it will discover them on its own
actionable: In prompts, explicitly name tools and agents you want used; don't assume Claude will find them without instruction
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Trust but verify: review generated code critically; give Claude self-verification tools (unit tests, linting, Playwright) to catch its own errors
actionable: Don't blindly accept Claude's first output; provide self-verification tools; review code before merging
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: AI can write tests that pass while the code is wrong (adjusts tests to match code instead of vice versa); developers must review both code and tests
actionable: Always review tests and code together; don't assume passing tests mean correct implementation
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently

claim: Developers should still write code manually for trivial changes; don't waste tokens on AI for minor edits (e.g., margin changes)
actionable: Use Claude for complex logic; handle trivial changes manually to preserve tokens and stay in control of the codebase
source_title: My top 6 tips & ways of using Claude Code efficiently
